content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/outputs/OutputTab.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 29
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-emptystate-titletext]:
Component <EmptyState> has a new prop `titleText` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <EmptyState> and determine if any should be moved to the `titleText` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <EmptyState>{content} other children</EmptyState>
  After:  <EmptyState titleText=>other children</EmptyState>

Incident context:
  componentName: EmptyState
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: EmptyState
Prop: titleText
To: React.ReactNode

Code context:
```
   28    ) : (
   29      <EmptyState variant={EmptyStateVariant.full}>
   30        <EmptyStateBody>

```

### Fix 2
Line: 20
Rule [sd-prop-value-pagesection-variant-light]:
The value "light" is no longer valid for the `variant` prop on <PageSection>.
Choose the appropriate replacement from the valid values: "default", "secondary"

Review the component documentation to determine which value best matches the behavior of "light".

Incident context:
  componentName: PageSection
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: light

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropValueChange
Component: PageSection
Prop: variant
From: light

Code context:
```
   19    return pipelineRun.status?.pipelineResults || pipelineRun.status?.results ? (
   20      <PageSection variant="light" isFilled>
   21        <ResultsList

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/outputs/OutputTab.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me reason through each fix: ### Fix 1 (Line 29): EmptyState `titleText` prop The `` component has a new `titleText` prop in v6. Looking at the current children: - `` containing a `

{t('No Output found')}

` The `EmptyStateBody` is body content, not title content. There's no `` or title-like content being passed as children. However, the `titleText` prop is meant to replace what was previously an `` or title. In this case, there's no explicit title — just body text. The text "No Output found" is semantically a title/heading for the empty state. In PF v6, `EmptyState` expects a `titleText` prop. The body content saying "No Output found" is really the title message. I should move it to `titleText` and since there's no other body content, remove the `EmptyStateBody`. Wait, let me reconsider. The text "No Output found" could be either a title or body text. In PF v6, `EmptyState` typically requires a `titleText`. Given that this is the only content and it reads like a title/status message, it makes sense to set it as the `titleText`. The `EmptyStateBody` would then be empty/unnecessary. ### Fix 2 (Line 20): PageSection `variant="light"` → `variant="default"` The value `"light"` is no longer valid. The valid values are `"default"` and `"secondary"`. The `"light"` variant in PF v5 corresponds to `"default"` in PF v6 (light background = default appearance). Let me also check if removing `EmptyStateBody` means I should remove it from imports.
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. All changes are applied properly. ## Changes Applied ### Fix 1 — `sd-new-absorbing-prop-emptystate-titletext` (Line 29) **Applied.** The `` had a single `` child containing `

{t('No Output found')}

`, which is semantically a title/heading message for the empty state. Moved this text to the new `titleText` prop on ``. Removed the now-unnecessary `` wrapper and its `

` tag. Also removed the `EmptyStateBody` import since it is no longer referenced. ### Fix 2 — `sd-prop-value-pagesection-variant-light` (Line 20) **Applied.** Changed `variant="light"` to `variant="default"` on ``. The `"light"` value is no longer valid in PF v6; `"default"` is the equivalent replacement (light background = default appearance).
